quotes.title: Emergent Abilities of Large Language Models

quotes.title: Emergent Abilities of Large Language Models

derived_research.name: The unverifiable-reward wall
derived_research.definition: On the hardest, highest-value tasks there is no reliable reward, reference, or oracle, so training signal, evaluation signal, and oversight fail together for one shared reason.

derived_research.name: Predict-before-you-build (the pre-deployment forecasting gap)
derived_research.definition: Governance, safety, and evaluation all require ex-ante forecasts of a model's capabilities AND failure modes before it is built or fielded, yet the corpus can predict loss, not behaviour.

derived_research.name: Mechanistic opacity as the upstream assurance gate
derived_research.definition: Every downstream guarantee (valid evaluation, alignment assurance, deception-detection, a correct explanation) is gated on reading the mechanism that produced a capability, which the corpus repeatedly calls unsolved.
derived_research.latency: partial (corpus half-names the mesa-optimizer + Sleeper Agents pair)

derived_research.name: The construct-validity crisis
derived_research.definition: As outputs become open-ended and models generalize, we can no longer guarantee a benchmark measures the intended construct rather than contamination, judge-model limits, or a metric artifact, undermining progress claims and the emergence signal used to forecast.

derived_research.name: Inheritance across the fine-tuning boundary
derived_research.definition: Fine-tuning, distillation, and adaptation are treated as capability transfer, but the corpus separately shows they inherit or reshape unwanted properties (memorized data, deception, value drift, forgetting) with no theory of what survives derivation.

derived_research.name: Superposition as a shared root of opacity and brittleness
derived_research.definition: Many features packed into fewer dimensions may be a common mechanistic cause of both uninterpretable internals and adversarial fragility (and the efficiency ceilings that motivate compression).
derived_research.latency: full but narrow (2 families at high substance)

derived_applied.name: Runtime governance
derived_applied.definition: Supervising, constraining, and assuring an AI system while it operates (at decision time), because it cannot be certified before deployment.
derived_applied.provenance: Meets all five derived-frontier criteria, and is the MOST strongly latent (the corpus never names it at all). Analyst-nominated (owner's runtime-governance ontology; Maurits Kaptein / kyvvu); not eligible for the blind pass because it was the seed. Classified applied, not research: an operational response to research frontiers, not an open research question. Shown in full as an equal member, tagged by type, not ranked as if it failed a test.
